text: Lambda calculus (Alonzo Church, 1936) is a formal system for expressing computation via function abstraction and application. It is Turing-complete and the theoretical foundation of functional programming (Lisp, Haskell, ML, Clojure, JavaScript's arrow functions).
Church numerals encode natural numbers: 0 = λf.λx.x, 1 = λf.λx.f x, 2 = λf.λx.f(f x).
